IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Wednesday, July 22, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — Top scale **$54.75/hr** and it's showing. Ermco's Google Data Center play in Mooresville is eating calls (21 JWs), and the IU Hospital twin calls through Industrial Electric and Electric Plus are solid 5-10s with Saturday 8s, all climate-controlled indoor work. Board's at 149 hands deep across 40 calls — this one's moving.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** — Lake Mariner Data Center is the diesel engine here. Ferguson (70 JWs) and O'Connell (39 JWs) are both running 7-10s at **$48.20/hr** with per diem available. That's 109 bodies on one project. Orientation cycles Mon/Tue/Thu keep the pipeline clean. 125-hand book, 8 total calls — tight local, fast placement.

**LU-401 Reno, NV** — Top scale **$57.39/hr** at Bombard and Baker. NEWTRON's STY Campus shop work hits **$100/day per diem** Mon-Fri, $200 weekends, capped at $700/week, plus 5-10s with OT expected. Fiber splicing and modular install experience moves you to the front. 146 hands, 23 calls.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — Data center stack: Gurtz, MJ Electric, and Cupertino all posting Port Washington. **JW wage plus $10/hr** overscale, 50-60 hour weeks, drug screens required. 191 hands competing for 37 calls means book depth — but the extra ten bucks per hour on top of Milwaukee scale is real money.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** — Inglett & Stubbs carrying 44 JWs at QTS Fayette, Wellstar Paulding Hospital taking 25 more at **$40/hr**. Miller's cable splicer work requires three years documented time. 254 hands on the board, 39 calls — solid saturation.

Data center buildouts are absorbing electricians coast to coast; the Midwest corridor (Milwaukee, Indianapolis, Reno) is where the overscale premiums are sticking.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Wednesday, July 22, 2026**

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** is running hot with 45 calls and roughly 132 hands needed — New River Elect. Corp. is dominating the board with 34 JW lineman slots on distribution work, **$44.67/hr** plus **$125/night per diem** and a **$100 bonus**. The same contractor's got 9 more across Southern Ohio at the same rate. Thayer Power & Comm. is adding 4 JW slots on VVO and reconduct with hotels covered and **$500-600 bonuses**. This is the volume play right now.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** shows 10 calls for roughly 104 hands — that's tight. Summit Line and Cupertino Electric are both firing at **$55/hr** (top scale) with all OT double time and NM State JIW License EE98J required. Rosendin's undercutting at **$40 + $15 incentive** but you're grinding 90% of a 60-hour week to hit it. This is inside work on projects — not distribution.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** has 26 calls chasing 75 hands on the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood — Parsons Electric's got 20 JW slots at **$44.80/hr**, **$100/day non-taxed per diem**, **$150 weekends**, plus an extra **$100/day if you grab your ND license**. Real ID required; Hunt Electric's running parallel slots at the Air Base.

**LU-1393 Indianapolis, IN** — 36 calls, 57 hands. ARC American's got 6 JW lineman rebuilds at **$64.66/hr** with no per diem, but MJ Electric's transmission work (345kv circuit install) in Winamac is **$67.18/hr** plus **$105/day**. T-line work with solid depth.

**LU-429 Nashville, TN** is lean at 8 calls but Newtron's Google Clarksville project needs 40 JW inside guys working 7-10s at **$120/day per diem** (50+ miles). That's the data-center boom pulling hands south.

Data-center infrastructure and transmission backbone work are absorbing the board — 450+ calls across 58 locals means the market's tight enough that scale and per diem separations matter hard.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Wednesday, July 22, 2026**

**LU-140 Salt Lake City, UT** is running the fattest board of the day—21 calls for 71 hands. BCS Mechanical's got 31 Other calls on a West Valley job (no pay posted, but journeyman rate sits at **$6.00/hr scale**), and ACCO's Cedar City gig is pushing **$6/hr plus $175/day per diem** if you stay the full week. DSI's Draper shop needs TIG-qualified fitters. This is volume work; book depth is real here.

**LU-776 Lima, OH** is the payday local. **$53.00/hr** Toledo rate plus incentives—Nooter's fab/Cenovus combo is running 6-10s with **$125/day per diem** on incentive, and BMWC's ethanol plant gig adds **$145/day per diem** for hands over 50 miles out. Graham's Cenovus maintenance needs current DISA certs on two pipefitters. Eight calls total, 25 hands—tight board, solid work.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** sits at 20 calls and 47 hands. Murphy's Corden Pharma Boulder runs **15% shift differential + 15% incentive pay** for fitters, plus **$125/day per diem** if you're outside 100 miles. US Eng. Metalworks is heavy fab shop (13 calls) for SS fitters with 15% shift diffe. Three skilled trades stacked here.

**LU-290 Portland, OR** is calling 20 hands for specialized work: Charter's running orbital welders at Intel Ronler Acres (Ticket #71459), PermaCold's commercial refrigeration, and MacDonald Miller's HVAC service tech slots. Thinner pay data but steady commercial/tech gigs.

**LU-213 St. John, NB** has 11 calls chasing Irving Oil Refinery work—Lorneville and Stuart Olson both bidding day/night shifts and 4x10s on GPMA terms. Day-to-night transitions mean real shutdown momentum.

Refinery and industrial process work is vacuuming up hands—Lima, Saint John, and Salt Lake are where the long-haul money is moving this week.
